### Post by DougieFresh4U on 2008-09-21
Is this some thing new with Intrepid:
```

# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# Note that some configuration settings that could be done previously
# in this file, now are automatically configured by the server and settings
# here are ignored.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Configured Video Device"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Configured Monitor"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Monitor		"Configured Monitor"
	Device		"Configured Video Device"
EndSection
```

That is all that comes up. Nothing about screen  or depth. I need to get at those to change

### Post by TheMono on 2008-09-21
It should theoretically be detected automatically, but the syntax is still the same as old xorg.conf's, so you can just add it in if you want to specify manually.

---

### Post by DougieFresh4U on 2008-09-21

Thanks, I understand what you are saying, but I can not get to the file to edit. The code only brings up what I have posted.

### Post by ronacc on 2008-09-21
you have to edit it as root , use "sudo gedit" . for most people the default you see works best but xorg will use an edited xorg.conf if it is there. try your old one from hardy if you have a copy .

I need 16 bit for a game to work as it keeps telling me that 16 bit is required and I am stuck with 8 bit.

### Post by drsaamah on 2008-09-21
I think we are confused as to what you are asking.  If you "sudo nano /etc/X11/xorg.conf" into the terminal it will *show* what you posted above.  From there you treat it just like a word processor and make the changes you want.  When you are done, hit "Control"+"X" and then it will allow to save the changes.
If this isn't your problem, then please be more clear.

```
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)

# in this file, now are automatically configured by the server and settings
# here are ignored.
```

```
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Device"
        Identifier      "Configured Video Device"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
        Identifier      "Configured Monitor"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
        Identifier      "Default Screen"

```
Before, you were able to have all the numbers (screen res) and it would show the depth number as well. Now all that comes up is "Configured Monitor"
I am sorry if I am not clear. I will search through old post and post whay I am looking for.

Right.  What you want to do is to add a
```

DefaultDepth 16

```
to the "Screen" section.

Although I'd actually guess that you've got a Wine problem rather than an xorg.conf problem; unless something is very messed up you will, by default, have a colourdepth of 24 bits (8 red + 8 green + 8 blue).

---

### Post by ronacc on 2008-09-21
that is the file you need to edit . the new xorg.conf is VERY sparse but you can add sections if you need to . you will need to atleast add a subsection "display" under the screen section like this .

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Default Screen"
    Device         "Generic Video Card"
    Monitor        "SyncMaster"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "AddARGBGLXVisuals" "true"
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       1
        Modes      "1440x900" "1280x1024" "1280x960" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       4
        Modes      "1440x900" "1280x1024" "1280x960" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       8
        Modes      "1440x900" "1280x1024" "1280x960" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       15
        Modes      "1440x900" "1280x1024" "1280x960" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       16
        Modes      "1440x900" "1280x1024" "1280x960" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
        Modes      "1680x1050"  "1440x900" "1280x1024" "1280x960" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400"
    EndSubSection
EndSection

you will want atleast 8 and 16 depths   Note! the modes are for my starlogic widescreen LCD and will be different for you monitor you will need to figure that out yourself  . hope this helps

---

### Post by DougieFresh4U on 2008-09-21
When code was executed before this is what USED to come up:

```
Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc101"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver		"synaptics"
	Option		"SendCoreEvents"	"true"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/psaux"
	Option		"Protocol"		"auto-dev"
	Option		"HorizEdgeScroll"	"0"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load "GLcore"
	Load "i2c"
	Load "bitmap"
	Load "ddc"
	Load "dri"
	Load "extmod"
	Load "freetype"
	Load "glx"
	Load "int10"
	Load "vbe"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Configured Video Device"
	Driver		"radeon"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Configured Monitor"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Monitor		"Configured Monitor"
	Device		"Configured Video Device"
	DefaultDepth 24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth 1
		Modes "1024x768"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth 4
		Modes "1024x768"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth 8
		Modes "1024x768"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth 15
		Modes "1024x768"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth 16
		Modes "1024x768"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth 24
		Modes "1024x768"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

```

And now all you get is the little bit that I have posted. 
I am not angry, but I am frustrated.

### Post by RAOF on 2008-09-21
It would probably be useful for you to post details of your *actual* problem - that problem seems to be that a game isn't running.  What game, from where, and with what version?  What's the full output of trying to run it?  Chances are that fiddling with xorg.conf is *not* going to fix the problem that you're having.

### Post by ronacc on 2008-09-21
if that is an old xorg.conf for your system try that , just rename your current xorg.conf to something else ( i use xorg.confo) and substitue the one you just posted and see what happens . worst case you can just delete the new one and rename your original back to xorg.conf . note I am using the exact same xorg.conf that I have been using since dapper .

### Post by ronacc on 2008-09-22
if you have an icon for it on your desktop you can rt click on the icon select properties then launcher and that should show the command you would type into the terminal to start the prg , or else try using the full path to the program .

### Post by Tatty on 2008-09-22
The shorter xorg has existed in ubuntu since hardy, as a result of improved autodetection in xorg.  This should explain it:

[https://help.ubuntu.com/community/XORGHardy](https://help.ubuntu.com/community/XORGHardy)
